textPlain: This is helpful for estimating how much higher display resolutions are still perceivable and when we enter the territory of "marketing bullshit".For example:- 40 cm view distance (e.g. smartphone): 300 ppi is roughly the maximum that's useful- 100 cm (e.g. desktop monitor): about 200 ppihttps://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-025-64679-2/figures/2
About 15 cm view distance for smartphones is pretty normal for me (shortsighted with glasses) on websites where the text is very small, e.g. Hacker News.
